# CTOS Portfolio Roadmap
# 06 — Authority Layer (RMD v1.0)

## Objective

Transform the portfolio from a showcase website into a credibility engine.

Authority is not built by claiming expertise.

Authority is built by demonstrating:

- Experience
- Systems Thinking
- Technical Decision Making
- Delivery Capability
- Real World Impact

---

# Authority Vision

Target Perception:

"This person understands systems beyond code."

Not:

"This person knows many technologies."

---

# Authority Pillars

## Pillar 01

Systems Architecture

Evidence:

- Infrastructure Projects
- Cloud Projects
- System Diagrams
- Architecture Decisions

---

## Pillar 02

Operational Capability

Evidence:

- Deployment Experience
- Homelab Experience
- VPS Experience
- Cloudflare Experience
- Troubleshooting Experience

---

## Pillar 03

Product Delivery

Evidence:

- Educational Ecosystem
- LMS
- CBT
- E-Learning
- E-Library
- RBAC Systems

---

## Pillar 04

AI Workflow Engineering

Evidence:

- PRD Systems
- Prompt Systems
- AI Workflows
- Automation Workflows

---

# Authority Success Criteria

A recruiter should be able to answer:

- What problems does he solve?
- What systems has he built?
- What decisions has he made?
- What impact did those decisions create?

Within 3 minutes.

---

# Sprint AU1 — Trust Signal Architecture

## Goal

Introduce credibility above the fold.

### Trust Signals

Years of Experience

Systems Managed

Projects Delivered

Infrastructure Experience

---

### Rules

Avoid vanity metrics.

Avoid inflated numbers.

Use verifiable evidence.

---

# Sprint AU2 — Flagship Project Strategy

## Goal

Create one dominant authority project.

### Primary Flagship

SMK Teknovo Ecosystem

---

### Narrative

Not:

"School Website"

Instead:

"Integrated Educational Platform Architecture"

---

### Required Sections

Overview

Problem

Architecture

RBAC

Infrastructure

Modules

Lessons Learned

Future Evolution

---

# Sprint AU3 — Case Study Authority Framework

## Goal

Turn projects into evidence.

### Structure

Challenge

Context

Requirements

Architecture Decisions

Implementation

Tradeoffs

Results

Retrospective

---

### Rule

Focus on decisions.

Not features.

---

# Sprint AU4 — Expertise Matrix

## Goal

Visualize capabilities.

### Categories

Infrastructure

Cloud

Automation

AI

Product Systems

Architecture

---

### Rules

No percentage bars.

No fake skill ratings.

Use capability statements.

---

# Sprint AU5 — Resume Authority System

## Goal

Modernize resume presentation.

### Sections

Executive Summary

Core Capabilities

Selected Projects

Education

Achievements

Contact

---

### Rule

Outcome Driven.

Not technology driven.

---

# Sprint AU6 — Certificate Vault Authority

## Goal

Turn certificates into supporting evidence.

### Metadata

Issuer

Date

Skills

Category

Description

Verification Link

---

### Categories

Cloud

Infrastructure

AI

Development

Security

Operations

---

### Rule

Certificates support expertise.

They do not define expertise.

---

# Sprint AU7 — Technical Notes Authority

## Goal

Build lightweight authority.

### Categories

Cloudflare

Infrastructure

Homelab

Automation

AI Workflow

Troubleshooting

Lessons Learned

---

### Content Rules

Short.

Practical.

Experience Based.

---

### Forbidden

Generic Tutorials.

Copied Content.

AI Generated Fluff.

---

# Sprint AU8 — GitHub Authority Layer

## Goal

Connect portfolio and repository ecosystem.

### Required Links

GitHub Profile

Featured Repositories

Flagship Repositories

---

### Repository Selection Criteria

Architecture Quality

Documentation Quality

Real World Utility

---

# Sprint AU9 — Professional Timeline

## Goal

Show growth without exposing sensitive details.

### Timeline Types

Learning Milestones

Project Milestones

System Milestones

---

### Avoid

Personal Information

Private Company Information

Sensitive Dates

---

# Sprint AU10 — Social Proof Layer

## Goal

Add trust without exaggeration.

### Sources

Projects

Deployments

Case Studies

GitHub Activity

Certificates

---

### Rule

Evidence First.

Claims Second.

---

# Sprint AU11 — Contact Authority Layer

## Goal

Create professional conversion path.

### Primary Contacts

LinkedIn

Email

GitHub

---

### CTA Style

Collaboration

Consultation

Architecture Discussion

Technical Partnership

---

# Sprint AU12 — Personal Brand Governance

## Goal

Maintain positioning consistency.

### Preferred Terms

Systems

Architecture

Infrastructure

Automation

Reliability

Operations

Integration

---

### Avoid Overuse

Expert

Guru

Ninja

Rockstar

---

# Authority Metrics

Recruiter Understanding

<3 minutes

---

Project Discovery

<60 seconds

---

Case Study Engagement

High

---

Resume Conversion

Trackable

---

# AI Agent Rules

Rule AU-01

Authority must come from evidence.

Rule AU-02

Projects must emphasize outcomes.

Rule AU-03

Case studies must explain decisions.

Rule AU-04

Avoid technology dumping.

Rule AU-05

Avoid inflated claims.

Rule AU-06

Maintain positioning consistency.

Rule AU-07

Every page must reinforce authority.

---

# Definition of Done

- Trust signals implemented.
- Flagship project completed.
- Case study framework completed.
- Expertise matrix completed.
- Resume authority system completed.
- Certificate vault completed.
- Technical notes structure completed.
- GitHub authority layer connected.
- Social proof layer implemented.
- Contact authority layer implemented.

---

# Next Layer

07-optimization-layer.rmd
